# Generated/MLClient

**Do not edit by hand. Regenerated by Kiota.**

This directory is the output of:

```sh
bash scripts/regen-contracts.sh dotnet
# (equivalent to: dotnet kiota generate -d ../../contracts/openapi.yaml \
#                                       -l CSharp -o src/ClimaSense.Web/Generated/MLClient \
#                                       -n ClimaSense.Web.Generated.MLClient \
#                                       -c MLApiClient --clean-output --exclude-backward-compatible)
```

Tooling pin: `microsoft.openapi.kiota` `1.31.1` (see `.config/dotnet-tools.json`).

It is also regenerated automatically on `dotnet build` by the
`KiotaRegenerate` MSBuild target in `ClimaSense.Web.csproj` (conditional
on Kiota being restored — see `BeforeTargets="BeforeBuild"`).

**Why these files are committed:** so the project builds for reviewers
who haven't installed Kiota yet. The regen script is idempotent — running
it on an unchanged contract produces zero diff against the committed
files.

**JSON naming policy:** the DTOs in `Models/` use Kiota's hand-rolled
serializers (`IParsable.GetFieldDeserializers` / `Serialize(ISerializationWriter)`),
which read/write the camelCase wire spelling directly. There are
**no `[JsonPropertyName]` attributes** anywhere in this tree — wire casing
is owned by Kiota's serializer machinery, not by attributes on POCOs.

**Runtime dependencies:** see `ClimaSense.Web.csproj`. The Kiota client
needs `Microsoft.Kiota.Abstractions`, `Microsoft.Kiota.Http.HttpClientLibrary`,
and the `Microsoft.Kiota.Serialization.*` packages.

**`.kiota.log` / `kiota-lock.json`:** committed alongside the generated
sources so a reviewer's regen matches exactly bit-for-bit. The lock file
also records the contract hash so accidental drift between the YAML and
the committed snapshot is obvious in `git diff`.
